title: microseris laciniata subsp. siskiyouensis
intro: Microseris laciniata subsp. siskiyouensis, also known as Siskiyou dune tansy or Siskiyou goldfields, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, colds, and fever. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, providing several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Microseris laciniata subsp. siskiyouensis typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used internally or applied topically to address various health issues.
